# cubrid-rs

> Native Rust database driver for CUBRID. Provides both sync and async clients that speak the CAS wire protocol directly over TCP. No FFI, no C dependencies - pure Rust.

cubrid-rs is a multi-crate Cargo workspace implementing the CUBRID CAS (Client Application Server) protocol in pure Rust. It provides `cubrid-client` (synchronous, std::net), `cubrid-tokio` (asynchronous, tokio), and `cubrid-pool` (connection pooling).

Key facts:
- DSN format: `cubrid://[user[:password]]@host[:port]/database`
- Sync client: `cubrid-client` crate
- Async client: `cubrid-tokio` crate
- Connection pool: `cubrid-pool` crate
- Protocol: `cubrid-protocol` crate (CAS wire protocol, I/O-agnostic)
- Features: pure Rust, #![deny(unsafe_code)], no FFI, no C dependencies
- Minimum Rust version: 1.75
- Status: v0.1.0 (scaffold - protocol implementation in progress)

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-rs/blob/main/README.md): Full documentation
- [PRD](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-rs/blob/main/docs/PRD.md): Product requirements with Example-first Design Philosophy
- [Architecture](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-rs/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): Multi-crate workspace design
- [Protocol Research](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-rs/blob/main/docs/PROTOCOL_RESEARCH.md): CAS wire protocol specification

## Examples

- [Sync Client](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-rs/blob/main/README.md#quick-start---sync-client): Synchronous database operations
- [Async Client](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-rs/blob/main/README.md#quick-start---async-client-tokio): Async database operations with tokio

## Ecosystem

- [pycubrid](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid): Pure Python DB-API 2.0 driver
- [cubrid-go](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-go): Pure Go database/sql driver
- [cubrid-client](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-client): TypeScript CAS client
- [cubrid-cookbook](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-cookbook): Production-ready examples
- [CUBRID](https://www.cubrid.org/): The CUBRID database
